design.Four years of industrial & systems engineering at NUS, focused on linear programming, optimisation strategies and simulation. Now pointing that same structure at brand, product and how things land with people.

SCROLL / DECODE
[01] SELF_INTRODUCTION

The analytical half,
looking for its other half.

I'm an Industrial & Systems Engineering graduate from NUS — specialising in Supply Chain Management with a minor in Management of Technology. Four years of training in modelling systems, cutting waste, managing timelines and budgets, and keeping cross-functional work aligned.

But the analytical side was never the whole picture. I'm deliberately building toward the other half — creative, product, brand and campaign work — where the same structured thinking gets pointed at how something looks, feels, and lands with people. This site is that balance in progress: an engineer's rigour, wearing a designer's skin.

Give me a messy problem with real constraints and a human on the other end of it, and I'll scope it, structure it, and make it land.

[02] WORK_EXPERIENCE

Where I've been the
coordination point.

Roles across procurement, operations and resource management — the common thread is being the person who keeps competing priorities and moving parts aligned.

Philips Pte Ltd Jan 2025 – Jun 2025 Logistics Procurement

Cross-functional procurement & supplier coordination

  • Acted as the coordination point between logistics, procurement, Quality, Legal and Business teams — aligning priorities and tracking milestones and deliverables to keep cross-functional work on schedule.
  • Ran supplier, pricing and cost analyses that fed directly into process-improvement decisions.
  • Managed supplier communications and contract administration end to end — drafting documentation, tracking compliance, and ensuring agreements executed accurately and on time.
Spearing Collectibles LLC Jun 2023 – Aug 2023 Operations Management

Vendor coordination & process improvement

  • Smaller team, fewer formal structures — the role demanded self-directed scoping and follow-through from start to finish.
MINDEF — Medical Corps Mar 2020 – Dec 2021 Resource Management

Onboarding, logistics & stakeholder coordination

  • Also owned a workplace redesign brief — sourcing suppliers and managing production through to installation (detailed under Work Projects).
[03] SCHOOL_PROJECTS

Academic work, from
simulation to strategy.

Engineering coursework and self-directed projects — where I move between hard modelling and consumer-facing thinking.

★ FEATUREDBEng Dissertation · IE4100R

Measuring Supply-Chain Disruption on Shipping Lead Times

Solo · AIS data analysis of Singapore ports · AY 2025/26

Using 2024 AIS vessel-tracking data, I measured how the Red Sea Crisis drove congestion and vessel delays across Singapore's ports — building a multi-metric congestion framework (berth utilisation, anchorage & turnaround time, arrival frequency, congested-vessel counts), evaluating Keppel Terminal's emergency reactivation, quantifying spillover to Port Klang and PTP, then running a service-failure analysis against pre-disruption baselines.

The finding: March–May congestion was demand-driven, not operational failure. Keppel's reactivation gave meaningful but partial relief, and no large-scale permanent diversion materialised — leaving an asymmetric resilience gap where Malaysian ports (especially PTP) stayed more exposed. Closed with four policy recommendations, including a data-calibrated 10–15% strategic buffer for Tuas Mega Port.

PythonAIS dataData cleaningCongestion modellingPearson correlation
41.12MTEUs — SG throughput, 2024
65→30%Keppel berth-util. relief
~27%excess demand Keppel absorbed
10–15%recommended Tuas buffer
VIEW POSTER ↗
P.01FlexSim · DES

NDCS Surgery — Discrete-Event Simulation

Team of 6 · IE3100R · FlexSim

Built a FlexSim DES model of the end-to-end Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery patient journey at the National Dental Centre — capturing rule-based doctor/chair assignment, patient routing and re-entrant follow-ups — then ran a chairs-vs-doctors sensitivity analysis on utilisation and wait times, leading to reminder-system and capacity-dashboard recommendations.

FlexSimDESSensitivity analysisHealthcare ops
P.02Livable Cities

Queenstown Liveability Study

Team of 6 · CDE2501

Diagnosed Singapore's first satellite town through a full SWOT, a systems map, and a sharpened problem statement — then set a liveability vision and design concepts targeting aging infrastructure, accessibility and fading local identity.

Systems mappingSWOTUrban design
P.03Facility Layout

Safety Driving Centre — Layout Optimisation

Facility Layout & Location

Applied From-To charts, REL charts and activity-relationship diagrams to diagnose service-flow inefficiencies at Singapore Safety Driving Centre, then proposed an optimised layout and staffing plan to improve customer experience.

From-To chartsREL chartsLayout design
P.04Brand Strategy

On Running — Brand Audit & Market Entry

Solo · Principles of Marketing

Owned end-to-end: audited On against Hoka and Adidas (SWOT, positioning, marketing mix), defined an "urban professional" segment, and built a Singapore go-to-market on lifestyle storytelling, sustainability and run-club activations.

Brand auditPositioningGo-to-market
P.05Product Concept

HaveFun Karaoke — Product & Brand

Group · Business Proposal

Led the concept for a mid-market karaoke franchise in Clementi — brand positioning ("Sing West, Sing Best"), service tiers, a guest-facing tech experience (AI pitch correction, QR self-service, cashless), and site-selection rationale.

Concept devService designSite selection
P.06MoT

GoPro — Technology Strategy & Disruption

Team · Management of Technology

Assessed whether GoPro can hold its edge against DJI, Insta360 and smartphones — mapping the action-camera S-curve across introduction, growth and maturity, stress-testing it through disruptive-innovation theory, and recommending an ecosystem-, software- and brand-led play (UGC identity, services, B2B) to defend a saturating high-end market.

Tech S-curvesDisruptive innovationCompetitive strategy
P.07Design Team

RC Hovercraft — Horizontal Thrust Lead

Team · engineering design

Led the horizontal-thrust system for an RC hovercraft carrying a 300g payload at 1 m/s with obstacle avoidance — sourced and tested components, and flagged tradeoffs early by liaising with the Electrical, Body and Vertical-lift teams to keep every sub-system in sync.

Component sourcingTestingDependency mgmt
P.08Product Concept · Live

SONDER — Eau de Parfum

Solo · Self-directed fragrance concept · 2026

A complete product build for a fictional fragrance — naming, composition narrative (black tea & bergamot / wet concrete & iris / vetiver & smoke), editorial campaign direction, and an interactive product page with a draggable 3D bottle. Brand thinking taken all the way to a shippable artefact.

Product conceptArt directionInteractive 3D

[04] WORK_PROJECTS

Owned end to end,
on the job.

Two projects where I ran the full arc — brief, sourcing, execution and handover — rather than a slice of it.

W.01Workplace Design

Lift & Office-Level Redesign

MINDEF · sourcing → installation

Took a signage refresh from brief to installed reality: scoped the work across a noticeboard, infographic poster frames and lift-door designs; sourced and coordinated suppliers; reviewed and approved production drafts; then oversaw on-site install and quality — signed off within budget and timeline.

ScopingSupplier sourcingProductionInstall & QA
W.02Procurement

Philips — Last-Mile Provider RFQ

Logistics procurement · sourcing

Ran a request-for-quotation across multiple suppliers to appoint Philips' last-mile provider — building the comparison of quotations and rates, and scoring each not just on cost but on delivery risk, so the recommendation was the cheapest option that was also the safest bet.

RFQCost vs riskSupplier selection
